oneOf.description: Initiates the bonding process for a user.

min_mint_amount.description: Minimum expected amount of LST tokens to be received for the operation to be considered valid.

mint_to.description: The address to mint the LST to.

oneOf.description: Initiates the unbonding process for a user.

amount.description:
The amount to unstake.

NOTE: In the original milkyway implementation, the contract expected funds to be sent to it (verified with must_pay) since the tokens were all native (ibc denoms for the base token, tokenfactory for the lst)

staker.description: The address that will receive the native tokens on.

batch_id.description: ID of the batch from which to withdraw.

staker.description: The address that will receive the funds.

withdraw_to_address.description: The address to withdraw the funds to on this chain.

new_owner.description: Address of the new owner on the protocol chain.

oneOf.description: Accepts ownership transfer; callable by the new owner.
Revokes ownership transfer; callable by the current owner.
Updates contract configuration; callable by the owner.

batch_period.description: Updated unbonding batch execution frequency (in seconds).

monitors.description: Updated list of circuit breaker monitors.

oneOf.description: Receives rewards from the native chain.
Receives unstaked tokens from the native chain.

batch_id.description: ID of the batch that originated the unstake request.

oneOf.description: Stops the contract due to irregularities; callable by monitors and admin.
Resumes the contract; callable by the admin.

total_bonded_native_tokens.description: Updated total native tokens delegated (used post-slashing).

total_issued_lst.description: Updated total issued liquid staked tokens.

Addr.description:
A human readable address.

In Cosmos, this is typically bech32 encoded. But for multi-chain smart contracts no assumptions should be made other than being UTF-8 encoded and of reasonable length.

This type represents a validated address. It can be created in the following ways 1. Use `Addr::unchecked(input)` 2. Use `let checked: Addr = deps.api.addr_validate(input)?` 3. Use `let checked: Addr = deps.api.addr_humanize(canonical_addr)?` 4. Deserialize from JSON. This must only be done from JSON that was validated before such as a contract's state. `Addr` must not be used in messages sent by the user because this would result in unvalidated instances.

This type is immutable. If you really need to mutate it (Really? Are you sure?), create a mutable copy using `let mut mutable = Addr::to_string()` and operate on that `String` instance.

Binary.description:
Binary is a wrapper around Vec<u8> to add base64 de/serialization with serde. It also adds some helper methods to help encode inline.

This is only needed as serde-json-{core,wasm} has a horrible encoding for Vec<u8>. See also <https://github.com/CosmWasm/cosmwasm/blob/main/docs/MESSAGE_TYPES.md>.

Bytes<HexPrefixed>.description: A string representation of bytes, encoded via HexPrefixed

ProtocolFeeConfig.description: Config related to the fees collected by the contract to operate the liquid staking protocol.

fee_recipient.description: Address where the collected fees are sent.

Uint256.description:
An implementation of u256 that is using strings for JSON encoding/decoding, such that the full u256 range can be used for clients that convert JSON numbers to floats, like JavaScript and jq.

# Examples

Use `from` to create instances out of primitive uint types or `new` to provide big endian bytes:

``` # use cosmwasm_std::Uint256; let a = Uint256::from(258u128); let b = Uint256::new([ 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 0u8, 1u8, 2u8, ]); assert_eq!(a, b); ```

Uint64.description:
A thin wrapper around u64 that is using strings for JSON encoding/decoding, such that the full u64 range can be used for clients that convert JSON numbers to floats, like JavaScript and jq.

# Examples

Use `from` to create instances of this and `u64` to get the value out:

``` # use cosmwasm_std::Uint64; let a = Uint64::from(42u64); assert_eq!(a.u64(), 42);

let b = Uint64::from(70u32); assert_eq!(b.u64(), 70); ```
